q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is os.remove(file_path) on line 364, which performs irreversible file deletion.

mock_guidance.target: Mock or record the os.remove call on line 364.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the file_path argument passed to os.remove to verify whether it contains path traversal sequences (e.g., '../').
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Do not actually delete any files. Either raise OSError to simulate 'file not found' or simply no-op, allowing the function to continue and return a JSON response.
notes: The tainted path is derived from os.path.join(UPLOAD_FOLDER, filename) on line 342.
The function returns different JSON responses depending on login state and whether the file is found, so the mock should allow normal control flow after the os.remove call.
